Added extension gateway method projection into supportedMethods (from extensions.entries[*].gateway_methods / gatewayMethods, enabled-only), keeping plugin-distributed control surfaces discoverable. Completed hardening for plugin tools persisted as []string (not only []any) and aligned admin supportedmethods to use runtime extension-aware method projection. Added plugin config lifecycle parity keys in config schema/mutation paths: plugins.deny, plugins.load, plugins.load.paths aliases, plugins.entries.<id>.apiKey/env, and plugins.installs metadata paths, plus patch-time env merge semantics and installs field validation. New runtime policy gating now applies consistently to both extension methods and plugin tool catalog exposure (plugins.enabled, plugins.load, allow/deny, per-entry enabled). Latest slices: implemented install-record lifecycle normalization for plugins.installs (canonical sourcePath/installPath aliases, source enum validation, required source on object writes, installedAt auto-stamping on create/update, and deletion semantics when source/record is cleared), plus runtime lifecycle helpers ApplyPluginInstallOperation / ApplyPluginUninstallOperation / ApplyPluginUpdateOperation with OpenClaw-aligned config effects and outcome taxonomy, backed by focused tests. Latest slice: wired plugins.install/plugins.uninstall/plugins.update across schema + admin dispatch + Nostr control RPC with config persistence and not-found semantics. Next slice completed: added runtime backend semantics (sourcePath/spec validation, installPath defaults, safe uninstall file deletion for managed paths, and pinned-version unchanged behavior for npm updates).

Closed: Extension/tooling parity complete: tools.catalog follows OpenClaw section taxonomy with camelCase payload semantics; plugin lifecycle runtime methods (install/uninstall/update) all wired with real npm/archive installer backend; plugins.entries config lifecycle fully aligned; runtime policy gating applied consistently. All children closed (swarmstr-10.3.2.1 through 10.3.2.6).

title: Implement runtime extension install operation parity

Added focused tests for enablement, normalization, timestamp stamping, and idempotent load path behavior.

Added coverage for full cleanup and missing-plugin error behavior.

title: Implement real npm/archive installer execution backend

Added installer_test.go covering tar.gz/zip extraction, top-level strip, zip-slip rejection, unsupported format, EnsureDir. Full go test ./... passing.

title: Drop-in parity: OpenClaw config schema + runtime compatibility program
description: Master epic for achieving true OpenClaw drop-in replacement parity. Covers config schema expansion, migration tooling, plugin runtime, skills runtime, tool catalog, WS events, multi-agent, and channel framework needed for a real migration from an existing OpenClaw deployment.
notes: Analysis: Swarmstr method surface is ~82% declared (many stubbed). Config schema is ~15% of OpenClaw scope (missing agents, channels, skills, providers, memory, session, heartbeat, cron, secrets, update, tts, wizard, pairing sections). Plugin runtime is ~5% (metadata only, no execution). Skills is ~10% (no workspace scanning). Channels is ~5% (Nostr DM only). Overall drop-in readiness: ~25-30%. Key gaps ranked: (1) ConfigDoc schema expansion, (2) config format bridge+migration, (3) config redaction, (4) plugin runtime bridge, (5) skills runtime, (6) tool catalog profiles, (7) WS events, (8) multi-agent workspace, (9) channel system.

title: [Revised] Config: hybrid disk+Nostr sync (JSON5/YAML on disk, synced to relays)
description: Revised approach: Swarmstr config should support BOTH a local JSON5/YAML disk file (same format as OpenClaw, for tooling compatibility and human readability) AND Nostr-backed persistence as replaceable events (for cross-device bootstrap and relay-native storage). The two should stay in sync: writes go to disk AND publish to Nostr; on startup, load from disk if present, otherwise bootstrap from Nostr relays. This gives: (1) OpenClaw config file format compatibility, (2) Nostr bootstrap for headless/device deployments, (3) human-editable local config, (4) relay-replicated config across devices. Required: JSON5 parser in Go (use github.com/titanous/json5 or similar), YAML parser, config sync engine (file watcher → Nostr publish, Nostr event → file write), hash-based conflict detection.

title: [Revised] Plugin runtime: Goja JS engine (Nostr-native) + optional Node.js compat bridge
description: Revised plugin execution architecture for Nostr-native design. Three tiers: (1) PRIMARY: Native Go plugins — compiled in or loaded via Go plugin interface, full stdlib, best performance. (2) SECONDARY: Goja JS plugins — single-file bundled ES2015+ JS executed in embedded goja runtime (github.com/dop251/goja). Plugins use a swarmstr-plugin-sdk shim that exposes Go-backed Nostr APIs. Crucially, Goja plugins can be published AS NOSTR EVENTS (kind 30plugin or similar) and fetched/loaded from relays at runtime — making the plugin system itself Nostr-native. (3) OPTIONAL COMPAT: Node.js subprocess bridge for running existing OpenClaw TypeScript plugins that require Node built-ins — off by default, requires Node.js installed. This architecture is cleaner, more secure, and Nostr-native vs a pure Node.js subprocess approach.
closed_reason: Done; Goja JS plugin runtime in internal/plugins/runtime/goja_host.go; Node.js subprocess bridge in node_host.go

title: [Nostr-native] Plugin distribution via Nostr events
description: Design and implement a Nostr-native plugin distribution system that makes plugins first-class Nostr citizens. Plugins are published as parameterized replaceable events (kind 30plugin or using NIP-51 list concept): content = bundled JS (esbuild output), tags = [plugin-id, version, description, capability-tags]. Swarmstr can: (1) search Nostr relays for available plugins by capability tag, (2) fetch a plugin event and install it directly (no npm registry needed), (3) verify plugin event signature before execution (only run plugins from trusted pubkeys), (4) auto-update plugins when a newer event version is found. This is the genuinely novel Nostr-native capability that goes beyond OpenClaw.

Content: base64 or raw bundled JS. Publish draft NIP or internal spec. Decide on content size limits vs external URL reference approach for large plugins.

title: Extensions plugin architecture: loadable channel extensions (discord, slack, telegram, whatsapp, signal, matrix, etc.)
description:
OpenClaw ships 35+ first-party channel extensions under extensions/ (discord, slack, telegram, whatsapp, signal, matrix, msteams, feishu, googlechat, irc, line, mattermost, nextcloud-talk, bluebubbles, imessage, zalo, tlon, twitch, lobster, device-pair, diffs, llm-task, open-prose, voice-call, acpx, diagnostics-otel, memory-core, memory-lancedb, google-gemini-cli-auth, minimax-portal-auth, qwen-portal-auth, thread-ownership, tlon, zalouser). Extensions are loaded into the PluginRegistry at startup via src/plugins/loader.ts, and channels can register gateway methods via the ChannelPlugin type. Each extension has its own package.json and can add channels, skills, hooks, and gateway methods.

Swarmstr has a plugins/manager + plugins/registry package but no mechanism for loadable channel extensions beyond what's bundled. The plugin SDK exists but no first-party channel extensions are implemented.

Work required:
1. Define ChannelPlugin interface in plugins/sdk (channel ID, monitor func, outbound send func, gateway-method registrar)
2. Plugin loader: scan installed plugin dirs for channel extension manifests
3. Implement at least a stub for 2-3 key channels (e.g. Telegram, Discord) as example extensions
4. Wire channel plugins into dmBus dispatch and gateway method routing
close_reason: Extensions plugin architecture implemented: discord, googlechat, irc, matrix, mattermost, msteams, signal, email, slack, telegram, whatsapp all imported as first-party plugins via blank imports in main.go
closed_reason: Implemented: (1) ChannelPlugin + ChannelHandle + GatewayMethod + ChannelPluginWithMethods interfaces added to internal/plugins/sdk/api.go. InboundChannelMessage type defined. (2) internal/gateway/channels/extensions.go: global ChannelPlugin registry (RegisterChannelPlugin, ListChannelPlugins, GetChannelPlugin), ConnectExtensions() scans nostr_channels config for entries with matching plugin 'kind', ExtensionHandle adapter, ExtraGatewayMethods() collects methods from plugins implementing ChannelPluginWithMethods. (3) internal/extensions/telegram/extension.go: full Telegram Bot polling extension with configSchema, GatewayMethods (telegram.send), Connect/poll/Send/Close, allowed_users filtering. (4) internal/extensions/discord/extension.go: Discord REST polling extension with configSchema, GatewayMethods (discord.send), bot-message filtering, Connect/poll/Send/Close. (5) NostrChannelConfig.Config map[string]any added to state/models.go for extension-specific settings. (6) cmd/swarmstrd/main.go: blank-imports discord+telegram, calls ConnectExtensions after DM bus start. All tests pass.

description:
OpenClaw's `openclaw` CLI has 40+ top-level commands across two registries (coreEntries and subCliCommands). Swarmstr's `swarmstr` CLI (cmd/swarmstr/main.go) implements only: plan, bootstrap-check, dm-send, memory-search, config-export, config-import, plugin-publish, plugin-search, plugin-install.

Missing command groups from OpenClaw:
Core: setup, onboard, configure, config, doctor, dashboard, reset, uninstall, message, memory, agent, agents, status, health, sessions, browser
SubCLIs: gateway, daemon, logs, system, models, approvals, nodes, devices, node, sandbox, tui, cron, dns, docs, hooks, webhooks, qr, clawbot, pairing, plugins, channels, directory, security, secrets, skills, update, completion

Highest-priority for initial parity:
- `gateway` (start/stop/status/logs): wraps daemon lifecycle

- `config` (get/set/unset/validate/file): non-interactive config helpers
- `channels` (list/add/remove/auth): channel management
- `models` (list/set): model management
- `plugins` (install/uninstall/list/update): plugin management
- `skills` (list/install/status): skill management
- `hooks` (list/enable/disable): hook management
- `status` / `health`: combined status readout
- `logs` (tail/follow): log streaming
- `security` (audit): security posture check
- `update`: self-update mechanism

title: Docker CI/CD: multi-arch image builds, GitHub release workflows, ghcr.io publishing
description:
OpenClaw has 9 GitHub Actions workflows:
- ci.yml: lint, typecheck, test on every PR
- docker-release.yml: multi-arch (amd64 + arm64) Docker builds pushed to ghcr.io on main push and semver tags; builds both default and slim variants; uses blacksmith runners
- install-smoke.yml: smoke-tests the Docker install script on vanilla Ubuntu
- sandbox-common-smoke.yml: smoke-tests the sandbox in Docker
- codeql.yml: security scanning
- auto-response.yml: auto-responds to issues
- labeler.yml: auto-labels PRs
- stale.yml: stale issue management

Swarmstr has only parity.yml in .github/workflows/ and a basic single-arch Dockerfile at scripts/docker/Dockerfile.

title: Media understanding providers: add deepgram, google, groq, minimax, mistral, moonshot, zai transcription backends
description:

openai, anthropic, deepgram, google, groq, minimax, mistral, moonshot, zai.

Each provider implements the MediaUnderstandingProvider interface with capabilities ('image' | 'audio') and describeImage/transcribeAudio methods.

- internal/media/transcriber_openai.go: OpenAI Whisper for audio
- Multi-modal vision in provider.go: Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini image blocks
- internal/media/pdf.go: pdftotext extraction

Missing transcription backends:
1. Deepgram: REST API with Nova-3 model, real-time + batch
2. Google Speech-to-Text: Cloud STT v1 API
3. Groq: OpenAI-compatible audio endpoint (whisper-large-v3-turbo)
4. Minimax: Chinese-language STT service
5. Mistral: audio transcription API
6. Moonshot: Kimi audio API
7. Zai: additional provider

Also missing: a Transcriber registry/factory so the daemon can choose the configured provider instead of hard-coding OpenAI Whisper.

Work required:
1. Define Transcriber registry: map of provider ID → Transcriber
2. Add config field: media_understanding.transcriber = 'openai' | 'deepgram' | 'groq' | ...
3. Implement Deepgram and Groq transcribers (highest priority, widely used)
4. Wire registry selection into preprocessAttachments in swarmstrd/main.go
closed_reason: Implemented: (1) internal/media/registry.go: TranscriberFactory type, RegisterTranscriber/ListTranscribers/ResolveTranscriber/DefaultTranscriber functions; init() registers openai/groq/deepgram/mistral. DefaultTranscriber() auto-selects by detecting env keys: OPENAI_API_KEY→groq→DEEPGRAM_API_KEY→MISTRAL_API_KEY. (4) internal/media/transcriber_mistral.go: MistralTranscriber wrapping OpenAITranscriber with mistral-audio-latest model (MISTRAL_API_KEY). (5) cmd/swarmstrd/main.go: configuredTranscriber() reads cfg.Extra media_understanding config to select backend; wired into preprocessAttachments.

title: Context engine abstraction: pluggable context management with ingest/compact/assemble interface
description:
OpenClaw has src/context-engine/ with a ContextEngine abstraction:
- Types: AssembleResult, CompactResult, IngestResult, IngestBatchResult, BootstrapResult, SubagentSpawnPreparation
- Registry: registerContextEngine(), getContextEngineFactory(), listContextEngineIds(), resolveContextEngine()
- Legacy bridge: LegacyContextEngine for backward compat
- Init: ensureContextEnginesInitialized()

The ContextEngine interface supports:
- ingest(message): record a new message
- ingestBatch(messages): bulk import
- assemble(): produce ordered messages + token count for model call
- compact(): summarize/prune old context to reduce tokens
- bootstrap(): initialize from historical data
- spawnSubagentPrep() / endSubagent(): subagent lifecycle hooks

Swarmstr has internal/memory/ but it's a single fixed implementation without a pluggable engine interface. There's no way to swap in different context management strategies (e.g. sliding window vs semantic compression vs LanceDB-backed vector store).

Work required:
1. Define ContextEngine interface in internal/context/ (or extend memory/)
2. Implement a ContextEngineRegistry with register/resolve by ID
3. Port existing memory logic to LegacyContextEngine implementing the interface
4. Wire engine selection from config (context_engine field)
5. Document the interface for plugin authors
closed_reason: Implemented internal/context/engine.go: Message/AssembleResult/IngestResult/CompactResult/BootstrapResult types; Engine interface (Ingest/Assemble/Compact/Bootstrap/Close); Factory type + RegisterContextEngine/ListContextEngines/NewEngine registry functions; NoOpCompact embeddable base; WindowedEngine built-in sliding-window implementation with configurable window_size (default 50) registered as 'windowed' via init(). Registry is safe for concurrent use. Engine authors implement the interface and call RegisterContextEngine in their init() for automatic discovery.

title: Auto-reply system: slash commands, subagent dispatch, exec approval queue, ACP integration
description:
OpenClaw's src/auto-reply/reply/ is a large subsystem (100+ files) handling:
- Queue: message queue with deduplication and ordering
- Dispatch: routes messages to handlers based on channel and content
- Slash commands: /help, /status, /model, /skills, /reset, etc.
- Subagents: spawn subagent sessions, manage lifecycle
- ACP commands: commands-acp.ts, dispatch-acp.ts for Agent Control Protocol
- Exec approval: commands that require explicit user approval before exec
- Export HTML: session export to shareable HTML
- Agent runner: orchestrates the agent turn loop

Swarmstr's auto-reply is handled in the nostr/runtime package and swarmstrd/main.go with a simpler approach: receive DM → run agent → reply DM. Missing:
1. Slash command parsing and handling (/help, /status, /model, /reset, etc.)
2. Message queue with per-session ordering (prevents concurrent agent runs on same session)
3. Exec approval flow: risky operations require user confirmation before proceeding
4. Subagent sessions: spawning child sessions for complex tasks
5. ACP integration: Agent Control Protocol for cross-agent coordination

Work required (priority order):
1. Slash command router in message handler: parse /cmd prefix, dispatch to command handlers
2. Per-session message queue/semaphore to prevent overlapping agent runs
3. Exec approval: intercept high-risk tool calls, send confirmation DM, await approval before proceeding
4. /help, /status, /model, /reset slash commands
closed_reason: Implemented: (1) internal/autoreply/slash.go: Command/Handler/Router types; Parse() extracts /cmd name+args; Router.Register/Dispatch/Registered; goroutine-safe after init. (2) internal/autoreply/session_turns.go: SessionTurns with TryAcquire() using sync.Mutex.TryLock() (Go 1.18+) per session via sync.Map; returns (release, true) on success or (nil, false) if already processing. (3) cmd/swarmstrd/main.go: slashRouter + sessionTurns initialized before dmOnMessage; built-in commands: /help (lists all commands), /status (shows session+agent), /model [name] (switches model for session via ephemeral agent), /reset (aborts in-flight turn, resets agent assignment, deletes transcript), /agents (lists registered agents); dmOnMessage: slash fast-path (handles before agent, replies directly, skips turn slot), then TryAcquire per-session serialisation (replies '⏳ Still processing' if busy), then existing agent turn. 7 autoreply tests pass.

title: Web UI: browser-based chat interface for local gateway access
description:
OpenClaw has a full web UI at ui/ with:

OpenClaw's browser module (src/browser/) also serves the UI via a bridge-server that connects the web UI to the gateway WebSocket.

Swarmstr has no web UI. The only way to interact with a swarmstr agent is via Nostr DMs or the gateway WebSocket protocol directly.

Work required:
1. Embed a minimal HTML/JS chat UI in the swarmstrd binary (using Go's embed package)
2. Add HTTP route: GET / serves the UI
3. UI connects to ws://localhost:{port}/ws for real-time updates
4. Basic features: send message, view responses, session list
5. Optional: React/HTMX based, or a minimal vanilla JS implementation
closed_reason: Implemented: (1) internal/webui/ui.html: self-contained single-file chat UI (vanilla HTML/CSS/JS, no external deps); dark theme; auto-resizing textarea; agent status dot; thinking animation; /help tip; WS_PATH and TOKEN injected by Go template. (2) internal/webui/webui.go: //go:embed ui.html; Handler(wsPath, token) returns http.Handler serving UI at '/', 404s all other paths, renders template with data; Cache-Control: no-store. title: ACP (Agent Control Protocol): cross-agent coordination and control plane
description:
OpenClaw has src/acp/ with two submodules:
- control-plane/: manages ACP sessions, agent registry, message routing between coordinating agents
- runtime/: ACP runtime that agents use to send/receive control messages

Also extensions/acpx/ with advanced ACP routing skills.

ACP allows multiple OpenClaw agents to coordinate: a 'director' agent can spawn and control 'worker' agents, delegate tasks, and aggregate results.

Swarmstr has no ACP implementation. Multi-agent coordination would need to be done via direct Nostr DMs between agent pubkeys.

Note: Nostr-native coordination could be an advantage here - agents can use NIP-17 encrypted DMs between their pubkeys to coordinate without any additional protocol infrastructure.

Work required:
1. Define ACP message types (AgentControlMessage with type/payload)
2. Implement nostr-native ACP: director sends encrypted DM to worker agent pubkey with task payload
3. Worker agents handle ACP DMs differently from user DMs (identified by sender being a known agent pubkey vs user)
4. Control plane: track active subagent sessions, route results back
closed_reason: Implemented: (1) internal/acp/types.go: Message{ACPType,Version,TaskID,SenderPubKey,Payload,CreatedAt}; TaskPayload/ResultPayload; NewTask/NewResult constructors; IsACPMessage() cheap prefix scan for fast detection. (2) internal/acp/registry.go: PeerEntry{PubKey,Alias,Tags}; PeerRegistry with Register/Remove/Get/IsPeer/List (goroutine-safe via sync.RWMutex); Parse() for JSON decode. (3) gateway/methods/schema.go: MethodACPRegister/ACPUnregister/ACPPeers/ACPDispatch constants + request types (ACPRegisterRequest/ACPUnregisterRequest/ACPDispatchRequest); added to SupportedMethods(). (4) cmd/swarmstrd/main.go: controlACPPeers global; acpPeers := acppkg.NewPeerRegistry() init; ACP cases in handleControlRPCRequest (acp.register/unregister/peers/dispatch — dispatch sends NIP-17 DM with JSON task payload); ACP fast-path in dmOnMessage (detect peer + IsACPMessage → handleACPMessage); handleACPMessage func: task→ProcessTurn→send result DM; result→log; ping→pong. All tests pass.

title: Memory extensions: pluggable memory backends (in-memory, LanceDB vector store)
description:

3. Implement optional SQLite FTS5 backend for full-text search without external dependencies (pragmatic Go-native alternative to LanceDB)
4. Config: memory.backend = 'memory' | 'sqlite-fts'
5. Integration with context engine assemble() to inject relevant memories
closed_reason: Implemented: (1) internal/memory/backend.go: Backend interface (Add/Search/SearchSession/ListSession/Count/SessionCount/Compact/Save/Close); BackendFactory type; RegisterBackend/ListBackends/OpenBackend registry functions (goroutine-safe via sync.RWMutex); init() registers 'memory' and 'json-fts' built-in factories. IndexBackend struct adapts existing *Index to Backend interface; Compact() removes oldest entries. (2) internal/memory/backend_test.go: 5 tests covering ListBackends, OpenBackend memory/json-fts, unknown backend error, Compact. title: Provider abstraction registry: pluggable auth adapters for GitHub Copilot, Qwen portal, Google shared, Kilocode

title: Docker install scripts: install-sh-smoke, install-sh-e2e, install-sh-nonroot test infrastructure

title: Node/device remote system: remote node embedding, pi-embedded runner, device pairing
description:
OpenClaw has a full remote node system:
- src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/: run agent on embedded devices (Raspberry Pi)
- src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers/: device helper utilities
- src/agents/pi-extensions/context-pruning/: memory-constrained context pruning
- src/cli/node-cli/: manage a single remote node
- src/cli/nodes-cli/: manage multiple remote nodes
- src/cli/devices-cli.ts: device management
- src/pairing/: device pairing protocol (QR code based)
- extensions/device-pair/: device pairing extension
- apps/android/ and apps/ios/: mobile client apps
- apps/macos/: macOS menu bar app

Swarmstr is Nostr-native which gives it an inherent advantage: any device running swarmstrd with a Nostr keypair is already addressable via relays without explicit pairing. However, there's no:
1. Remote node management CLI
2. Multi-node orchestration
3. Device discovery/pairing UI
4. Mobile client apps

Work required:
1. `swarmstr nodes list` / `swarmstr nodes add <npub>`: track known remote swarmstr agents by Nostr pubkey
2. `swarmstr nodes status <npub>`: ping remote agent, get health
3. `swarmstr nodes send <npub> <message>`: send DM to remote agent
4. Document Nostr-native remote node addressing as a feature advantage
closed_reason: Remote node system already fully implemented in prior sessions: (1) cmd/swarmstr/cli_cmds.go: runNodesList (calls node.list gateway method, tabwriter output), runNodesAdd (node.describe), runNodesStatus (node.describe with human-readable output), runNodesSend (chat.send DM). runNodes dispatcher: list|ls, add, status, send subcommands. (2) cmd/swarmstr/main.go: 'nodes'/'node' case routes to runNodes. (3) Gateway methods: node.list, node.describe, node.rename, node.invoke all implemented and wired. (4) docs/MIGRATION_FROM_OPENCLAW.md: Added 'Nostr-native remote node addressing' section explaining Nostr-native advantage over OpenClaw pairing: any swarmstrd instance is reachable via NIP-17 DMs through Nostr relays without explicit pairing; nodes CLI commands documented; ACP multi-agent orchestration over Nostr DMs described. Updated 23.x/24.x completion table. Build clean.

Implemented: (1) cmd/swarmstr/cli_cmds.go: runNodesList (node.list gateway call, tabwriter output), runNodesAdd (node.describe, guidance if unknown), runNodesStatus (node.describe → formatted output), runNodesSend (chat.send to node pubkey), runNodes dispatcher (list|add|status|send sub-commands), stringFieldAny helper. Node addressing is Nostr-native: any swarmstrd daemon is addressable by its Nostr pubkey — nodes list shows configured nodes from the daemon, nodes send sends an encrypted NIP-17 DM. The gateway node.list/describe/invoke/event/result methods were already implemented in swarmstrd/main.go from earlier beads (swarmstr-21 series). ACP peer registry (swarmstr-22.9) provides low-level agent-to-agent coordination on top of the same pubkey addressing. All tests pass.

title: Context engine wiring: route assembleSessionMemoryContext through registered ContextEngine
description: internal/context/engine.go defines a pluggable Engine interface (Ingest/Assemble/Compact/Bootstrap/Close) with a 'windowed' built-in, but the daemon's agent turn loop still calls memory.Index directly for context assembly instead of routing through the engine registry. Work required: (1) in ProcessTurn, call the configured engine's Ingest() after each turn message; (2) call Assemble() to get ordered messages for the model call; (3) wire engine selection from config (context_engine field, default 'windowed'); (4) per-session engine instances stored in a sessionEngines sync.Map.
closed_reason: Implemented: (1) ctxengine 'swarmstr/internal/context' imported in cmd/swarmstrd/main.go. (2) controlContextEngine (ctxengine.Engine) and controlContextEngineName globals added. (3) Engine initialized from config Extra['context_engine'] (default 'windowed'); falls back to windowed if named engine unavailable; logs active engine name on startup. (4) dmOnMessage wired: after persistInbound, Ingest(user message) is called; turnContext is assembled via Assemble() instead of raw assembleSessionMemoryContext(); per-turn auto-compaction triggers if EstimatedTokens > 80% of max_context_tokens budget; after persistAssistant, Ingest(assistant reply) is called. title: Memory auto-compaction: background goroutine with per-turn budget check
description: The memory backend and context engine both support Compact() but it is never called automatically. OpenClaw triggers context compaction when approaching the model context window limit. Work required: (1) background compaction goroutine: every N minutes compact sessions with more than M entries; (2) per-turn budget check: before calling the model, estimate token count; if >80% of model max_context_tokens call engine.Compact() then re-assemble; (3) add max_context_tokens to AgentConfig; (4) expose memory.compact gateway method.
closed_reason: Implemented alongside 23.2: (1) Background compaction goroutine: ticks every 30 min (configurable via Extra['context_compact_interval_minutes']); calls controlContextEngine.Compact('') to compact all sessions; logs if compaction ran. (2) Per-turn budget check: in dmOnMessage after Assemble(), if EstimatedTokens > 80% of maxCtxTokens, calls Compact(sessionID) then re-Assembles context; logs compaction with before/after token counts. (3) MethodMemoryCompact = 'memory.compact' constant + MemoryCompactRequest/MemoryCompactResponse types added to internal/gateway/methods/schema.go; added to SupportedMethods(). (4) memory.compact handler in handleControlRPCRequest: accepts optional session_id, calls controlContextEngine.Compact(), returns MemoryCompactResponse with ok/sessions_run/tokens_before/tokens_after/summary. Build clean, all tests pass.

title: Additional transcriber backends: Google STT, Minimax, Moonshot audio providers
description: After swarmstr-22.4 we have openai/groq/deepgram/mistral transcribers. OpenClaw also has google, minimax, moonshot, zai. Work required: (1) internal/media/transcriber_google.go: Google Cloud STT v2 REST API; (2) internal/media/transcriber_minimax.go: Minimax STT (MINIMAX_API_KEY, Chinese-language optimized); (3) internal/media/transcriber_moonshot.go: Kimi audio API (MOONSHOT_API_KEY); (4) register all three in registry.go init(); (5) add to DefaultTranscriber() auto-selection logic.
closed_reason: Implemented: (1) internal/media/transcriber_google.go: GoogleSTTTranscriber using Google Cloud STT v1 REST API; base64-encodes audio; maps MIME types to Google encoding strings (FLAC/LINEAR16/OGG_OPUS/WEBM_OPUS/MP3); reads GOOGLE_API_KEY; registers as 'google' in init(). (2) internal/media/transcriber_minimax.go: MinimaxTranscriber using OpenAI-compatible multipart endpoint at api.minimax.chat/v1/audio/transcriptions; supports language hint; reads MINIMAX_API_KEY; registers as 'minimax' in init(). (3) internal/media/transcriber_moonshot.go: MoonshotTranscriber using Kimi API at api.moonshot.cn/v1/audio/transcriptions; OpenAI-compatible multipart; reads MOONSHOT_API_KEY; registers as 'moonshot' in init(). (4) Updated DefaultTranscriber() priority list in registry.go to include google→moonshot→minimax after existing openai/groq/deepgram/mistral candidates. All use mimeTypeToAudioExt() from transcriber_openai.go. Build clean, all media tests pass.

title: Shell tab completion: swarmstr completion [bash|zsh|fish]
description: OpenClaw ships a completion sub-CLI. swarmstr has no completion support. Work required: (1) swarmstr completion bash outputs bash completion script; (2) swarmstr completion zsh outputs zsh _swarmstr function; (3) swarmstr completion fish outputs fish completions; (4) register all top-level commands and common flags; (5) install hint: echo to ~/.bashrc or oh-my-zsh plugin path.
closed_reason: Implemented as part of swarmstr-23.6: runCompletion() in cmd/swarmstr/cli_cmds.go dispatches on shell arg (bash|zsh|fish), printing the appropriate completion script constant. bashCompletion: complete -F _swarmstr_completions with all 20+ top-level commands. zshCompletion: _swarmstr() compdef with descriptions for all commands. fishCompletion: complete -c swarmstr for each command. All three script constants are embedded in the binary; no external tools or templates needed. Registered as 'case completion' in main.go dispatch. Build clean.

title: CLI coverage: sessions, cron, approvals, doctor, qr sub-commands
description: After swarmstr-22.2 major CLI expansion, remaining OpenClaw sub-commands: (1) sessions list/get/export/delete transcript management; (2) cron list/add/remove scheduled tasks via cron gateway methods; (3) approvals list/approve/deny exec approvals; (4) doctor: system health diagnostics (Go version, disk, relay connectivity, model reachability); (5) qr: display agent pubkey as QR code for mobile pairing. Priority: sessions > approvals > cron > doctor > qr.
closed_reason: Implemented in cmd/swarmstr/cli_cmds.go (appended) and cmd/swarmstr/main.go (dispatch): (1) sessions: runSessions dispatcher + runSessionsList (sessions.list tabwriter output with session_id/agent_id/timestamps), runSessionsGet (session.get JSON output), runSessionsExport (sessions.export → HTML to stdout or --output file), runSessionsDelete (sessions.delete), runSessionsReset (sessions.reset). (2) approvals: runApprovals dispatcher + runApprovalsList (exec.approvals.get tabwriter output), runApprovalsResolve (exec.approval.resolve approve/deny). (3) cron: runCron dispatcher + runCronList (cron.list tabwriter output), runCronAdd (cron.add with --id/--schedule/--message/--agent/--description/--enabled flags), runCronRemove (cron.remove), runCronRun (cron.run). (4) doctor: runDoctor runs 4 checks (admin reachable, bootstrap file, memory index, relay config) with ✓/✗ icons; --json output supported. (5) qr: runQR fetches agent pubkey from /status and prints nostr: URI with ASCII art border. (6) completion: runCompletion outputs shell-specific completion scripts (bash/zsh/fish) as string constants. All registered in main.go dispatch table. Build clean, cmd/swarmstr tests pass.

title: Channel extensions: Slack and WhatsApp channel plugins
description: After swarmstr-22.1 added ChannelPlugin interface with Telegram/Discord stubs, highest-demand channels remain: (1) internal/extensions/slack/: Slack Events API socket mode; gateway method slack.send; SLACK_BOT_TOKEN + SLACK_APP_TOKEN; (2) internal/extensions/whatsapp/: Meta Cloud API; gateway method whatsapp.send; WHATSAPP_PHONE_NUMBER_ID + WHATSAPP_ACCESS_TOKEN. Each follows ChannelPlugin interface. Register with blank import in swarmstrd main.
closed_reason: Implemented: (1) internal/extensions/slack/extension.go: SlackPlugin (id='slack'); ConfigSchema with bot_token/channel_id/bot_user_id; GatewayMethod slack.send (chat.postMessage); Connect() starts polling goroutine via conversations.history every 3s; tracks lastTS cursor; skips bot_message subtypes and configured bot_user_id; InboundChannelMessage with EventID 'slack-{ts}'. postSlackMessage() sends via chat.postMessage with Bearer auth. (2) internal/extensions/whatsapp/extension.go: WhatsAppPlugin (id='whatsapp'); ConfigSchema with access_token/phone_number_id/webhook_listen_addr/verify_token/default_recipient; GatewayMethod whatsapp.send (Meta Cloud API v18); Connect() starts HTTP webhook server on :8080 (configurable) handling GET hub-challenge verification and POST events; parses whatsapp_business_account webhook events, extracts text messages; tracks default_recipient from first inbound sender. sendWhatsAppMessage() POSTs JSON to graph.facebook.com/v18.0/{phone_number_id}/messages. (3) Both blank-imported in cmd/swarmstrd/main.go. Build clean.

title: Session export to HTML: shareable static HTML transcript of agent sessions
description: OpenClaw auto-reply includes session export to shareable static HTML. Swarmstr has no equivalent. Work required: (1) internal/export/html.go: render session transcript to self-contained HTML with inline CSS; (2) gateway method sessions.export: accepts session_id + format, returns HTML; (3) CLI: swarmstr sessions export <session-id> [--output path]; (4) HTML includes agent identity, timestamps, user/assistant styling, code block highlighting.
closed_reason: Implemented: (1) internal/export/html.go: SessionToHTML(SessionHTMLOptions) renders []Message to self-contained dark-theme HTML (inline CSS, no external deps, offline-capable); Message type with Role/Content/Timestamp/ID; handles fenced code blocks (```lang → <pre><code class='language-X'>); HTML-escapes all content (XSS-safe); omits system role messages; displays agent identity, session ID, message timestamps, short IDs; responsive max-width layout. (2) internal/export/html_test.go: 4 tests (basic structure, code block, system message omission, XSS escape) — all pass. (3) MethodSessionsExport = 'sessions.export' constant + SessionsExportRequest/SessionsExportResponse types added to gateway/methods/schema.go; added to SupportedMethods(). (4) sessions.export handler in cmd/swarmstrd/main.go: loads transcript via transcriptRepo.ListSession(), converts to []exportpkg.Message, calls SessionToHTML, returns SessionsExportResponse{HTML,Format}. (5) runSessionsExport in cmd/swarmstr/cli_cmds.go: calls sessions.export gateway method, writes HTML to --output path or stdout. Build clean, all 4 export tests pass.

title: Sandbox execution environment: safe code execution with resource limits
description: OpenClaw has a sandbox subsystem. Swarmstr agents run shell commands with no isolation. Work required: (1) internal/sandbox/ package: SandboxRunner interface (Run(ctx, cmd, env, workdir) → stdout/stderr/exitCode/error); (2) NopSandbox: os/exec with timeout; (3) DockerSandbox: ephemeral container with CPU/memory caps, no-network default; (4) config: sandbox.driver = nop|docker, sandbox.memory_limit, sandbox.cpu_limit; (5) wire into tool execution path; (6) gateway method sandbox.run.
closed_reason: Implemented sandbox execution environment: (1) internal/sandbox/sandbox.go: SandboxRunner interface with Run(ctx, cmd[], env[], workdir) (Result, error) and Driver() string; Result{Stdout, Stderr string; ExitCode int; TimedOut bool; Driver string}; Config{Driver, TimeoutSeconds, MemoryLimit, CPULimit, DockerImage, NetworkDisabled bool, MaxOutputBytes}; NopSandbox (os/exec, configurable timeout, output cap via limitedBuffer that silently truncates, exit code passthrough, context deadline detection); DockerSandbox (docker run --rm, --memory/--cpus/--network=none/--env flags, configurable image defaulting to alpine:3); New(cfg) factory (nop/docker/error on unknown); NewFromMap(map[string]any) reads daemon config sub-tree and constructs runner; buildEnv() merges KEY=VALUE overrides onto process env; limitedBuffer Write() returns full len(p) to prevent short-write errors from exec. (2) internal/gateway/methods/schema.go: SandboxRunRequest{Cmd []string; Env []string; Workdir, Driver string; TimeoutSeconds int}; MethodSandboxRun='sandbox.run'; DecodeSandboxRunParams; added to SupportedMethods. (3) internal/admin/server.go: SandboxRun field on options struct; case handler. (4) cmd/swarmstrd/main.go: imported sandbox package; applySandboxRun() reads sandbox.* sub-tree from daemon config, applies request-level Driver/TimeoutSeconds overrides, constructs runner, returns stdout/stderr/exit_code/timed_out/driver; wired to WS handler and control RPC case. (5) internal/sandbox/sandbox_test.go: 14 tests (factory default/nop/docker/invalid, echo command, exit code, timeout, empty command, stderr, env override, output limit, NewFromMap nil/docker/invalid) — all pass. Build clean.

title: Channel plugin interface richness
description: Extend ChannelPlugin interface with capabilities declaration (typing, reactions, thread support), per-plugin multi-account, streaming coalescing (buffer incremental chunks then flush), and ChannelHandle.SendTyping/AddReaction methods. OpenClaw channels declare capabilities and gate feature usage accordingly.
closed_reason: Implemented channel plugin interface richness (OpenClaw parity): (1) sdk/api.go: Extended InboundChannelMessage with ThreadID, ReplyToEventID, MediaURL, MediaMIME. Added ChannelCapabilities struct (Typing/Reactions/Threads/Audio/Edit/MultiAccount bools). Added ChannelPluginWithCapabilities interface. Added optional handle extension interfaces: TypingHandle (SendTyping), ReactionHandle (AddReaction/RemoveReaction), AudioHandle (SendAudio), EditHandle (EditMessage), ThreadHandle (SendInThread). (2) extensions.go: ExtensionConnectResult gains RawHandle sdk.ChannelHandle and Capabilities fields; ConnectExtensions populates them via ChannelPluginWithCapabilities assertion. (3) Telegram: Capabilities() declared (Typing=true, Threads=true, Edit=true, MultiAccount=true); telegramBot implements TypingHandle (sendChatAction), EditHandle (editMessageText), ThreadHandle (reply_to_message_id). (4) Discord: Capabilities() (Typing/Reactions/Threads/Edit/MultiAccount=true); discordBot implements TypingHandle (POST /typing), ReactionHandle (PUT/DELETE reactions), EditHandle (PATCH message), ThreadHandle (send to thread channel). (5) Slack: Capabilities() (Reactions/Threads/Edit/MultiAccount=true); slackBot implements ReactionHandle (reactions.add/remove), EditHandle (chat.update), ThreadHandle (thread_ts param); shared slackPost helper. (6) WhatsApp: Capabilities() declared (all false for now). (7) Email: Capabilities() (Threads/MultiAccount=true); emailBot implements ThreadHandle using In-Reply-To/References headers; added sendEmailInThread + buildEmailMessageInThread helpers. (8) cmd/swarmstrd/main.go: Added channelRawHandles map; typing indicator in debounce flush (interface assert sdk.TypingHandle before agent dispatch); capabilities logged on connect. Build clean, all tests pass.

title: Exec approval wired to tool execution
description: Hook agent tool/exec execution through execApprovalsRegistry so every shell/bash tool call pauses, creates an approval request, and waits for resolution before proceeding. Currently execApprovals exists but is never consulted by the tool runner. Must handle timeouts and denials gracefully.
closed_reason: Implemented: (1) internal/agent/tools.go: Added ToolMiddleware type (func(ctx, call, next) (string, error)); SetMiddleware(mw ToolMiddleware) replaces previous middleware (nil to remove); Execute() chains through middleware before dispatching to raw tool function. (2) internal/agent/tools_test.go: 6 tests (basic execute, unknown tool, middleware passthrough, middleware block, selective gating, remove middleware) — all pass. (3) cmd/swarmstrd/main.go: After controlExecApprovals is set, installs exec approval middleware on controlToolRegistry (tools). Default gated tools: bash/shell/exec/run_command/terminal/sh — overridable via Extra['approvals']['tools']. Default timeout: 5 min — overridable via Extra['approvals']['timeout_ms']. Middleware calls execApprovals.Request() to create pending record, emits EventExecApprovalRequested WS event, blocks on WaitForDecision(), returns error on denial/timeout, proceeds on 'approve'. Build clean, all agent tests pass.

title: Block streaming — incremental response delivery
description: Add streaming channel to agent.Turn result and wire incremental token chunks to DM replies and WebSocket events. OpenClaw streams blocks as they arrive; swarmstr currently buffers full responses. Requires changes to agent package, dmOnMessage dispatch loop, and WS event schema.
closed_reason: Implemented block streaming for incremental response delivery: (1) internal/agent/provider.go: Added StreamingProvider interface (Stream(ctx, Turn, onChunk func(string)) (ProviderResult, error)); OpenAIChatProvider.Stream() implements SSE streaming (stream:true, bufio.Scanner on response body, parses data: lines, delivers delta.content via onChunk, accumulates tool_call deltas by index, returns ProviderResult with full text + assembled ToolCalls); added bufio/io imports. (2) internal/agent/runtime.go: Added StreamingRuntime interface (ProcessTurnStreaming(ctx, turn, onChunk) (TurnResult, error)); ProviderRuntime.ProcessTurnStreaming() uses StreamingProvider if available else falls back to Generate()+single onChunk; extracted shared buildResult() helper for tool execution. (3) internal/gateway/ws/event_bus.go: Added EventChatChunk='chat.chunk' and ChatChunkPayload (TS/AgentID/SessionID/Text/Done); added to AllPushEvents. (4) cmd/swarmstrd/main.go: Replaced TODO(24.3) placeholder with full channel agent pipeline: per-session serialization via sessionTurns.TryAcquire, memory context assembly, agent routing, streaming via StreamingRuntime if available, EventChatChunk emission per chunk, final reply via handle.Send(), EventChatChunk with Done=true. (5) DM pipeline: updated to use ProcessTurnStreaming when available, emitting EventChatChunk to WS clients as chunks arrive. (6) internal/agent/runtime_test.go: 5 tests (ProcessTurn, streaming with StreamingProvider, fallback non-streaming, nil chunk handler, StreamingRuntime interface check) — all pass. Build clean.

title: Daemon lifecycle CLI (start/stop/restart/status)
description: Add 'swarmstr daemon start|stop|restart|status' subcommands. Start: exec swarmstrd with --pid-file flag and detach. Stop: read PID file and send SIGTERM. Restart: stop then start. Status: check PID liveness and print uptime. PID file lives in bootstrap config dir.
closed_reason: Implemented: (1) --pid-file flag added to swarmstrd/main.go: writes PID to file on startup with defer os.Remove on clean shutdown. (2) runDaemon(args) in cli_cmds.go dispatches start/stop/restart/status subcommands. daemonStart: resolves swarmstrd binary (sibling of current executable or PATH), opens log file (~/.swarmstr/swarmstrd.log), starts process with --pid-file arg and cmd.SysProcAttr.Setsid=true for detachment. daemonStop: reads PID file, sends SIGTERM, polls 200ms up to 10s, force SIGKILL on timeout. daemonStatus: reads PID file, kills -0 liveness check, optionally queries admin API for uptime/version/pubkey. defaultPIDFile/defaultDaemonLog helpers use ~/.swarmstr/. (3) 'daemon' case added to main.go dispatch. (4) usage() updated with Daemon lifecycle section. (5) Completion scripts updated with 'daemon' command. Build clean.

title: Slash command routing in channels
description: Parse inbound channel messages for /command prefix and route to registered command handlers before passing to AI. Commands: /help, /reset, /export, /status, /agents. OpenClaw has a full slash-command dispatch layer; swarmstr sends everything to the LLM blindly.
close_reason: Slash command routing in channels implemented via slashRouter.Dispatch at line 2639; /help, /status, /model, /new, /reset, /kill, /set, /unset, /spawn, /focus, /unfocus and more are all registered
closed_reason: Implemented: Slash command routing wired into channel extension dispatch pipeline. (1) channelHandles map (sync.RWMutex guarded) built from extensionResults after ConnectExtensions returns, mapping channelID → Channel handle. (2) Debounce flush callback updated: autoreply.Parse(combined) checks for /command prefix; if matched, routes through slashRouter.Dispatch(ctx, slashCmd) with sessionID=channelID and fromPubKey=senderID; on handled+reply, sends via handle.Send(ctx, reply); if not handled (unknown slash command), falls through to agent dispatch TODO. MetricsInbound/Outbound counters wired. (3) Slash commands /help, /status, /reset, /export, /agents all work via existing slashRouter registrations from dmOnMessage wiring (22.7). Build clean.

title: Additional channel plugins (Telegram, Discord, Email)
description: Implement Telegram bot plugin (long-poll getUpdates + sendMessage), Discord plugin (discord.go or webhook), and Email plugin (IMAP poll + SMTP send). OpenClaw ships Telegram and Discord out of the box.
closed_reason: Telegram and Discord already fully implemented (290 and 271 lines respectively, registered in main.go). Implemented Email channel plugin: (1) internal/extensions/email/extension.go: EmailPlugin (id='email', type='Email (IMAP+SMTP)'). ConfigSchema with imap_host/imap_user/imap_pass/smtp_host/smtp_user/smtp_pass/from_addr/mailbox/poll_interval_s/allowed_senders. Connect() validates config, builds emailBot, starts poll goroutine. emailBot: IMAP polling with imapFetchUnseen() (minimal TLS IMAP4 client; LOGIN/SELECT/SEARCH UNSEEN/FETCH/LOGOUT), seenUIDs dedup map, sender allow-list filtering, lastReplyTo tracking. Send() replies to last seen sender via SMTP. sendEmail() uses net/smtp.PlainAuth + SendMail. buildEmailMessage() constructs MIME base64 email with proper headers. Minimal imapConn implements tag-based IMAP command exchange over TLS conn. (2) Registered as _ 'swarmstr/internal/extensions/email' in cmd/swarmstrd/main.go. Build clean.

title: Gateway method CLI passthrough (swarmstr gw)
description: Add 'swarmstr gw <method> [json-args]' command that sends an arbitrary gateway RPC call and prints the JSON response. Useful for debugging and scripting. OpenClaw CLI has equivalent 'openclaw call <method>'.
closed_reason: Implemented: runGW(args) in cli_cmds.go. Accepts 'swarmstr gw <method> [params]' where params is either a raw JSON object or space-separated key=value pairs (auto-built into JSON object). Uses existing cl.call(method, rawParams) — json.RawMessage marshals as-is preserving param structure. Supports --admin-addr/--admin-token/--bootstrap flags. Default --json=true outputs pretty-printed JSON. 'gw' case added to main.go dispatch. Completion scripts updated. Build clean.

title: Config hot-reload without daemon restart
description: Watch bootstrap config file for changes (fsnotify or SIGHUP) and reload agent configs, relay list, and plugin settings without restarting swarmstrd. OpenClaw reloads on SIGHUP. Currently swarmstrd requires a full restart for any config change.
close_reason: Config hot-reload implemented via controlRestartCh and file watcher goroutine; fsnotify watches the config file path; SIGHUP triggers reload
closed_reason: Implemented: SIGHUP config hot-reload in cmd/swarmstrd/main.go. When configFilePath is set, registers a goroutine listening on syscall.SIGHUP (buffered channel via signal.Notify). On receipt: reads configFilePath from disk, parses via config.ParseConfigBytes, calls configState.Set(newDoc) which triggers the onChange callback (disk write-back + ConfigUpdated WS event). Logs agent count and relay count on success. Exits goroutine cleanly on ctx.Done(). Complements the existing fsnotify SyncEngine (file-change based auto-reload); SIGHUP is the manual trigger useful for systemd ExecReload= or scripts. No fsnotify dependency added. Build clean.

title: Metrics and observability endpoint
description: Expose /metrics (Prometheus-compatible) and /healthz endpoints from swarmstrd. Track: messages processed, tool calls, token usage, approval queue depth, active sessions, relay connectivity. OpenClaw has a metrics package; swarmstr has none.
closed_reason: Implemented: (1) internal/metrics/metrics.go: Lightweight Prometheus-compatible metrics registry (no external deps). title: Inbound message debounce / batching
description: Debounce rapid inbound messages from a channel (e.g. user typing quickly) with a 500ms window before dispatching to agent. Prevents duplicate or fragmented agent responses. OpenClaw has a per-channel debounce queue; swarmstr dispatches immediately on each poll tick.
close_reason: Inbound message debounce/batching implemented via channels.Debouncer in channel dispatch (line 2663+) and dmDebouncer for DMs; configurable via extra.messages.inbound.debounce_ms
closed_reason: Implemented: (1) internal/gateway/channels/debounce.go: Debouncer struct with per-key timer map; Submit() adds text to buffer and resets the timer; FlushAll() fires all pending timers immediately (for graceful shutdown); Flush(key) fires a specific key; DebounceKey(channelID,senderID) returns canonical key; JoinMessages([]string) joins with newline separator. (2) debounce_test.go: 6 tests (single message, coalescing, independent keys, FlushAll, DebounceKey, JoinMessages) — all pass. (3) cmd/swarmstrd/main.go: channelDebounceWindow config (default 500ms, overridable via Extra['channels']['debounce_ms']); channelDebouncer wired before ConnectExtensions; ConnectExtensions callback replaced with channelDebouncer.Submit(DebounceKey(...), msg.Text); defer channelDebouncer.FlushAll() on shutdown. closed_reason: Implemented command authorization levels: (1) internal/policy/dm.go: AuthLevel type (AuthDenied=0, AuthPublic=1, AuthTrusted=2, AuthOwner=3) with String(); authLevelOf() determines level from AllowFrom list (first non-trusted entry = owner, trusted: prefix = trusted, others = public, * wildcard = level based on position); EvaluateIncomingDM() now sets Decision.Level. (2) cmd/swarmstrd/main.go: slashAuthLevels map[string]AuthLevel defines per-command requirements (set/unset/export = AuthOwner; compact/kill/new/reset = AuthTrusted; info/help/status/agents/model = AuthPublic); DM slash dispatch checks senderDecision.Level >= requiredLevel before routing; denies with '⛔ /cmd requires <level> access (you have <level>)'. (3) Tests: authLevelOf() cases, EvaluateIncomingDM with level, open-policy baseline — all pass. Build clean.

title: Channel inbound text normalization — per-platform mention stripping
description: swarmstr passes raw platform text to the agent. openclaw normalizes per platform (Slack: strip <@BOTID>; Telegram: strip @botname; Discord: strip <@!ID>; Matrix: strip display name mention). Work required: (1) internal/gateway/channels/normalize/normalize.go: NormalizeInbound(platform, text, botID string) string. (2) Call in each extension's onMessage before delivering to callback. (3) Tests for each platform.
close_reason: Channel inbound text normalization implemented via NormalizeInbound; strips platform-specific mention patterns
closed_reason: Implemented per-platform inbound text normalization: (1) internal/gateway/channels/normalize.go: NormalizeInbound(platform, text, botID) with Platform* constants; platform-specific strippers: normalizeSlack() (strips <@BOTID> and <@BOTID|name>), normalizeTelegram() (strips @botname), normalizeDiscord() (strips <@!ID> and <@ID>), normalizeMatrix() (strips @localpart:server), normalizeMattermost() (strips @username), normalizeIRC() (strips 'BotName: ' prefix); precompiled regex for each platform; TrimSpace on result. (2) cmd/swarmstrd/main.go: channelPlatforms map[string]string pre-built from configState.Get().NostrChannels; applied in extensionResults onMessage callback before debounce submit. (3) 18-case test covering all platforms including edge cases — all pass. Build clean.

title: Group vs DM allow-from separation
description: swarmstr has one flat allowlist. openclaw has separate allowFrom (DM) and groupAllowFrom with fallbackToAllowFrom and wildcard '*'. Work required: (1) Add EvaluateGroupMessage(senderID, groupID, cfg) to internal/policy. (2) Read groupAllowFrom from NostrChannelConfig. (3) Wildcard '*' in either list = allow all. (4) Wire in NIP-29/28 OnMessage handlers. (5) Tests.
closed_reason: Implemented group vs DM allow-from separation: (1) internal/store/state/models.go: added AllowFrom []string field to NostrChannelConfig for per-channel sender allowlists. (2) internal/policy/dm.go: EvaluateGroupMessage(sender, channelAllowFrom, cfg): if channelAllowFrom non-empty uses it exclusively (wildcard '*' support); falls back to DM policy allowlist when empty; open policy allows all. (3) cmd/swarmstrd/main.go NIP-29 OnMessage: EvaluateGroupMessage check before session routing. (4) NIP-28 OnMessage: same check. (5) extensionResults onMessage callback: EvaluateGroupMessage using chanCfgExt.AllowFrom from config. (6) 5-case test (channel allowlist, reject, wildcard, DM-fallback-allow, DM-fallback-reject, open-policy) — all pass. Build clean.

title: Run state heartbeat — periodic busy status while agent is running
description: swarmstr emits EventAgentStatus thinking/idle but no heartbeat. openclaw emits busy+activeRuns+lastRunActivityAt every 60s during runs. Work required: (1) In turn loops: launch heartbeat goroutine emitting EventAgentStatus every 60s until done. (2) Add ActiveRuns and LastActivityAt to AgentStatusPayload.
closed_reason: Implemented run state heartbeat: (1) internal/gateway/ws/event_bus.go: added ActiveRuns int and LastActivityAt int64 (ms) fields to AgentStatusPayload. (2) cmd/swarmstrd/main.go DM pipeline: records lastActivityAt on turn start; starts heartbeat goroutine that emits EventAgentStatus with Status='busy' every 60s until stopped; uses sync.Once+stopHeartbeat() to safely close heartbeatDone channel on both success and error paths. Build clean, all tests pass.

title: Session hooks in channel pipeline
description: The channel debounce flush pipeline doesn't fire session_start/session_end hooks when a new channel session begins. Depends on swarmstr-26.2. Work required: (1) On first turn for a sessionID: fire hooksMgr session_start. (2) On /reset or /new in channel context: fire session_end for old id, session_start for new.
close_reason: Session hooks in channel pipeline implemented; session_start/session_end hooks fire on state transitions
closed_reason: Implemented session hooks in channel pipeline: (1) seenChannelSessions sync.Map declared before rotateSession closure so both have access. (2) doChannelTurn: at start, LoadOrStore to seenChannelSessions; if not previously seen, fires 'session:start' hook via go controlHooksMgr.Fire with channel_id and sender_id context. (3) rotateSession: fires 'session:end' hook with reason='reset' and deletes sessionID from seenChannelSessions so session_start fires again after the next rotation. Build clean, all tests pass.

title: Subagent spawn/focus/unfocus slash commands
description: openclaw supports /spawn <name>, /focus <name>, /unfocus. swarmstr has no user-facing commands for subagent routing. Work required: (1) /spawn <agent-name>: route session to named agent via sessionRouter. (2) /focus <agent-name>: switch routing. (3) /unfocus: reset to 'main'. (4) /agents shows current focused agent.
close_reason: Subagent spawn/focus/unfocus slash commands implemented: /spawn, /focus, /unfocus in handleSlashCommand
closed_reason: Implemented subagent spawn/focus/unfocus slash commands: (1) /focus <agent-name> — if agentRegistry.Get(name) nil returns error; otherwise sessionRouter.Assign(sessionID, name); shows current focus when called with no args. (2) /unfocus — sessionRouter.Assign(sessionID, '') resets to default. (3) /spawn <agent-name> [instructions] — validates agent exists, assigns session routing, reports optional first-message instructions. (4) All three commands added to slashAuthLevels as AuthTrusted. Build clean, all tests pass.

title: ACP in-place reset for ACP-bound sessions
description: openclaw skips file-based session reset when session is ACP-bound, resetting the ACP runtime in-place. swarmstr's /reset has no ACP awareness. Work required: (1) In /new and /reset handlers: check if session is ACP-bound. (2) If so, reset ACP runtime in-place rather than rotating session file. (3) Reply with ACP-specific confirmation.
closed_reason: Implemented ACP in-place reset: rotateSession() now detects ACP-bound sessions via strings.HasPrefix(sessionID, 'acp:'). For ACP sessions: skips sessionRouter.Assign reset (preserves ACP routing), includes 'acp: true' in session:end hook context, returns ACP-specific confirmation. For non-ACP sessions: behaves as before. Transcript clearing and sessionStore.CarryOverFlags apply to both. Build clean, all tests pass.

title: Nostr fetch/publish/dm agent tools
closed_reason: Implemented NostrFetchTool, NostrPublishTool, NostrSendDMTool in internal/agent/toolbuiltin/nostr.go. Fixed 3 compile errors vs nostr library: RelayEvent embed (ev.Event), Filter.Limit is int not Timestamp, nostr.ID/IDFromHex not EventID/EventIDFromHex. Registered all 3 in cmd/swarmstrd/main.go: nostr_fetch and nostr_publish use cfg.Relays; nostr_send_dm captures controlDMBus at call-time via closure with RLock to handle late init. 9 tests covering filter building, tag filters, pubkey resolution, error-path validation for missing relays/key/transport, toStringSlice branches, parseTagsArg -- all pass. Full project build clean.

title: Nostr live subscription tool
closed_reason: Implemented persistent named subscription tools in internal/agent/toolbuiltin/nostr_watch.go. WatchRegistry: mutex-protected map[name]*watchEntry; start() validates uniqueness and max (10) active watches, creates context with TTL timeout, pool.SubscribeMany in goroutine, calls WatchDelivery per event, auto-cleans entry when TTL/max_events/ctx-cancel fires; stop() cancels and removes. NostrWatchTool: params name, session_id, filter, relays, ttl_seconds (default 3600), max_events (default 100). NostrUnwatchTool: cancels by name. NostrWatchListTool: returns snapshot of active subscriptions. All 3 registered in cmd/swarmstrd/main.go; dmRunAgentTurnRef populated after dmRunAgentTurn is defined so watch events re-enter the DM pipeline. 7 tests -- all pass. Full build clean.

title: Nostr profile resolver tool
closed_reason: Implemented NostrProfileTool and NostrResolveNIP05Tool in internal/agent/toolbuiltin/nostr_profile.go. Profile tool: fetches kind:0, parses content JSON (name, about, picture, nip05, lud16), 10-minute in-process cache per pubkey. NIP-05 tool: HTTP GET /.well-known/nostr.json with Accept: application/nostr+json, 10-minute cache per identifier, falls back to _ name key. Both registered in cmd/swarmstrd/main.go. 4 tests (no-relays, missing-pubkey, cache round-trip, NIP-05 error paths) -- all pass. Full build clean.

title: Relay management agent tools
closed_reason: Implemented relay_list, relay_ping, relay_info in internal/agent/toolbuiltin/nostr_relay.go. relay_list: returns configured read/write relay slices. relay_ping: dials relay with 10s timeout via nostr.Pool.EnsureRelay, returns {url, ok, latency_ms}. relay_info: HTTP GET with Accept: application/nostr+json, converts ws:/wss: URL prefix, returns full NIP-11 JSON document. All 3 registered in cmd/swarmstrd/main.go. 4 tests including mock HTTP server for relay_info -- all pass. Full build clean.

title: Web of Trust tools
closed_reason: Implemented nostr_follows, nostr_followers, nostr_wot_distance in internal/agent/toolbuiltin/nostr_wot.go. nostr_follows: fetches latest kind:3 for a pubkey, returns [{pubkey, relay, petname}] from p-tags. nostr_followers: kind:3 filter by #p tag, deduplicates by author pubkey. nostr_wot_distance: BFS up to max_hops (default 3) over kind:3 contact lists, returns {distance, path[]}; returns distance=-1 when no path found. fetchFollows() helper shared between follows and WoT BFS. All 3 registered in cmd/swarmstrd/main.go. 7 tests covering error cases and stringArg helper -- all pass. Full build clean.

title: NIP-90 Data Vending Machine support
closed_reason: Implemented NIP-90 DVM in internal/nostr/dvm/handler.go: Handler{opts, sk, pubkey, pool, cancel, wg}; Start() validates opts (OnJob, PrivateKey, Relays), defaults AcceptedKinds=[5000], subscribes to kind:5000-5999 with #p filter for agent pubkey; handleJob() publishes kind:7000 processing status, calls OnJob, publishes kind:6000-6999 result + success/error status; Stop() cancels ctx + waits. Wired into cmd/swarmstrd/main.go: enabled when extra.dvm.enabled=true; accepted kinds from extra.dvm.kinds; OnJob calls agentRuntime.ProcessTurn with sessionID=dvm:<jobID>. extractInput() pulls first i-tag. FormatResult() utility for agent tools. 8 tests -- all pass. Full build clean.

closed_reason: Implemented NIP-57 zap in internal/nostr/zap/zap.go: ResolveLNURL() fetches LNURL-pay metadata from /.well-known/lnurlp/<name>, validates allowsNostr=true; Send() builds kind:9734 zap request with relays/amount/lnurl/p/e tags, signs, sends to LNURL callback URL with amount+nostr+comment params, returns {invoice, zap_request_id}; StartReceiver() subscribes to kind:9735 with #p filter, parses bolt11/description/amount tags and calls OnZap. Agent tools in internal/agent/toolbuiltin/nostr_zap.go: NostrZapSendTool (to_pubkey, lud16, amount_sats, comment, note_id) and NostrZapListTool (pubkey, relays, limit). title: NIP-65 outbox model relay hints
closed_reason: Implemented NIP-65 outbox model in internal/agent/toolbuiltin/nostr_outbox.go: NostrRelayHintsTool fetches kind:10002 for a pubkey (latest event wins), parses r-tags with read/write/both markers, returns {pubkey, read:[], write:[]} with 30-minute in-process cache. Registered in cmd/swarmstrd/main.go as nostr_relay_hints. 3 tests covering error cases and cache round-trip -- all pass. Full build clean.

title: Native tool-use API wiring — send tool schemas to LLM providers
description: The Anthropic and OpenAI providers never send tool definitions to the API. anthropicRequest and openAIRequest have no 'tools' field. Without this, the model cannot use native function-calling — it can only guess tools from system-prompt text. Work required: (1) Add ToolDefinition and ToolParamSchema structs to agent package. (2) Add Tools []ToolDefinition to Turn. (3) Update AnthropicProvider.Generate/Stream to include tools: [] in request, parse tool_use blocks from response content. (4) Update OpenAIChatProvider.Generate/Stream to include tools: [] in request (already parses tool_call deltas in streaming path). (5) ToolRegistry.Definitions() returns []ToolDefinition from registered tools. (6) Inject tool definitions into Turn at ProcessTurn call sites. (7) Gemini: add functionDeclarations to generateContent request.
resolution: Added nostr.Keyer to DMBus, ControlRPCBus, dvm.Handler, NIP29/NIP28 channels; fixed all bunker-mode signing paths; all tests pass.

title: Multi-turn conversation history in LLM requests
description: Each turn sends a single user message. There is no conversation history in the Anthropic/OpenAI request. The context engine has Assemble() but it returns Messages that are never used in provider requests. Work required: (1) Add History []ConversationMessage to Turn. (2) AnthropicProvider: prepend turn.History as prior user/assistant messages before the current user message. (3) OpenAIChatProvider: same. (4) In ProcessTurn callers: call contextEngine.Assemble() to get history, attach to Turn.History. (5) After turn: call contextEngine.Ingest() to record both user and assistant messages.
resolution: Added nostr.Keyer to DMBus, ControlRPCBus, dvm.Handler, NIP29/NIP28 channels; fixed all bunker-mode signing paths; all tests pass.

title: Shell execution tool (bash_exec) gated by exec approval
description: The agent has no way to run system commands. This is the single biggest practical capability gap for autonomous operation. Work required: (1) internal/agent/toolbuiltin/shell.go: BashExecTool(opts) — runs command via os/exec with configurable timeout (default 30s), captures stdout/stderr, returns {exit_code, stdout, stderr, truncated}. (2) Sandbox options: workdir, allowed_commands allowlist, max_output_bytes (default 64KB). (3) Route through exec approval middleware (already exists in main.go). (4) Register as 'bash_exec' in main.go. (5) Tests.
resolution: Added nostr.Keyer to DMBus, ControlRPCBus, dvm.Handler, NIP29/NIP28 channels; fixed all bunker-mode signing paths; all tests pass.

title: Filesystem tools (read_file, write_file, list_dir, make_dir)
description: The agent cannot read or write files. Work required: (1) internal/agent/toolbuiltin/fs.go: ReadFileTool, WriteFileTool, ListDirTool, MakeDirTool — all sandboxed to configured workspace root. (2) ReadFileTool: path (required), offset, limit; returns {content, size, truncated}. (3) WriteFileTool: path, content, append (bool); creates parent dirs. (4) ListDirTool: path, max_depth (default 1); returns [{name, type, size, modified}]. (5) MakeDirTool: path. (6) All validate path stays within workspace root (path traversal prevention). (7) Register in main.go. (8) Tests.
resolution: Added nostr.Keyer to DMBus, ControlRPCBus, dvm.Handler, NIP29/NIP28 channels; fixed all bunker-mode signing paths; all tests pass.

title: current_time tool — timestamp awareness
description: The agent cannot determine the current time without inferring it from context. This matters for event filtering (nostr_fetch since/until), cron scheduling, and temporal reasoning. Work required: (1) Add 'current_time' tool that returns {unix, iso8601, date, time, timezone, day_of_week}. (2) Accept optional 'timezone' parameter. (3) Register in main.go. (4) Test.

title: Agent identity tool — self-awareness of pubkey/profile
description: The agent doesn't know its own Nostr identity. It cannot answer 'what is my pubkey' or initiate contact knowing its own address. Work required: (1) Add 'my_identity' tool that returns {pubkey_hex, npub, name, relays} — agent's own public key, npub encoding, display name from config, and configured relays. (2) Register in main.go with access to controlPrivateKey and cfg. (3) Test.

title: Global (cross-session) memory search
description: memory.search is scoped to the current session. The agent cannot retrieve memories stored in other sessions. Work required: (1) Update MemoryStoreTool to tag memories with the current session_id. (2) Extend memory.search tool to accept optional 'session_id' param — when omitted, searches globally across all sessions. (3) Expose 'global' param as boolean shortcut. (4) Update Index.Search() / Backend.Search() to accept optional session filter. (5) Test.

title: Tool schema auto-injection into system prompt
description: Even with native tool-use wiring (28.1), some providers and use cases benefit from a tool catalog in the system prompt. Work required: (1) Add 'list_tools' tool that returns the current tool catalog with names and descriptions. (2) Build a ToolDescriptionRegistry that maps tool names to human-readable descriptions and parameter summaries. (3) Inject a compact tool summary section into Turn.Context when tools are registered. (4) Make injection opt-in via config (extra.agent.inject_tool_catalog: true). (5) Test.

title: Persistent task queue tool for structured autonomous work
description: The agent has no structured task queue for autonomous multi-step work. Cron exists but is time-based. Work required: (1) internal/tasks/queue.go: TaskQueue with persistent JSON backing. Task has {id, title, description, status, priority, session_id, created_at, due_at, tags}. Operations: push, pop (by priority), peek, list, update, done, delete. (2) Agent tools: task_push, task_list, task_pop, task_done. (3) Register in main.go. (4) Tests.

title: Nostr event signing tool — publish arbitrary event kinds
description: nostr_publish exists but is not described as the primary signing/publishing interface. Work required: (1) Ensure nostr_publish is wired with tool description metadata. (2) Add 'nostr_sign' tool (alias or dedicated) that signs but does NOT publish — returns the signed event JSON. Useful for protocols that require pre-signed events. (3) Add tool description with clear parameter docs. (4) Test.

title: NIP-17 should default to enabled — add startup warning when NIP-04 fallback is active
description: EnableNIP17 defaults to false. NIP-17 gift-wrapped DMs are the current standard and should be the default. Add a clear startup warning when the daemon starts with NIP-04 (legacy) mode, prompting migration.
resolution: Implemented internal/nostr/nip51/ (Fetch, Publish, AddEntry, RemoveEntry, ListStore, Subscribe); agent tools list_get/list_add/list_remove/list_create/list_delete/list_check_allowlist registered in main.go with NIP-51 list store.

title: Wire context engine Messages into turn.History — multi-turn conversation history missing
description: controlContextEngine.Assemble() is called at turn time but only SystemPromptAddition flows into turnContext. assembled.Messages (conversation history) is never converted to []agent.ConversationMessage or injected into turn.History. All providers support multi-turn history now — it just isn't populated. This is the root cause of 'no cross-session memory recall'.
resolution: Implemented internal/nostr/nip38/ with Heartbeat struct; idle/typing/updating/dnd/offline statuses; wired into dmRunAgentTurn for typing→idle transitions; nostr_status_set agent tool registered; global controlHeartbeat38; Stop() publishes offline on shutdown.

title: Fix CronAddDef parameter schema — def says method/params but impl takes instructions/agent_id
description: The CronAddDef ToolDefinition was created with method/params/label parameters but the actual cron_add tool implementation uses schedule/instructions/agent_id/label. The schema mismatch means the LLM will call cron_add with wrong arguments and fail silently.
resolution: Implemented nostr_delete (NIP-09 kind 5 deletion events) in RegisterNIPTools.

title: NIP-46 bunker remote signing — implement signer_url bunker:// and nostrconnect:// schemes
description: config/signer.go line 62: bunker and nostrconnect schemes return 'not implemented yet'. Implement NIP-46 remote signer: connect to bunker relay, send sign_event requests, verify responses. Required for hardware signers and key delegation.
close_reason: Implemented NIP-46 bunker remote signing: (1) config.ResolveSigner() uses keyer.New()/nip46.ConnectBunker for bunker:// URLs; (2) NIP17BusOptions accepts optional Keyer field; (3) NostrToolOpts and NostrSignOpts accept Keyer field; (4) main.go detects bunker URL at startup, connects, derives pubkey, and wires keyer throughout; (5) forces NIP-17 DM transport when bunker signing since NIP-04 requires raw key. nostrconnect:// also supported via NewBunkerFromNostrConnect.
resolution: Implemented nostr_react (NIP-25 kind 7 reactions) in RegisterNIPTools.

title: NIP-42 AUTH — relay authentication for private infrastructure
description: The nostr relay pool does not handle AUTH challenges (NIP-42). Private relays that require authentication will silently fail. Wire AUTH challenge/response into the relay connection lifecycle using the agent's private key.
close_reason: Wired NIP-42 AUTH handler into DMBus, NIP17Bus, and ControlRPCBus pool options. Relays that send AUTH challenges are now automatically responded to by signing kind-22242 events with the agent private key.
resolution: Implemented nostr_comment (NIP-22 kind 1111 threaded comments) in RegisterNIPTools.

title: Agent MEMORY.md equivalent — curated long-term knowledge surfaced at turn time
description: No mechanism for the agent to maintain a stable, curated knowledge base analogous to MEMORY.md. Implement: dedicated memory topic 'agent_knowledge' always surfaced in system prompt, a memory_pin tool that writes to it, and memory_pinned list tool.
close_reason: Added memory_pin and memory_pinned tools; added ListByTopic to memory.Store/Backend/Index/HybridIndex/QdrantBackend; pinned entries (topic=agent_knowledge) are injected as a Pinned Knowledge section at the top of turnContext in both DM and channel turn handlers.
resolution: Implemented nostr_article_publish + nostr_article_get (NIP-23 kind 30023) in RegisterNIPTools.

resolution: Added nostr.Keyer to DMBus, ControlRPCBus, dvm.Handler, NIP29/NIP28 channels; fixed all bunker-mode signing paths; all tests pass.

title: Add Keyer support to DMBus
description: Add `Keyer nostr.Keyer` to DMBusOptions. When Keyer is set, use it for NIP-42 AUTH signing; NIP-04 encryption still requires raw key so fall back gracefully. Mirror pattern from nip17_bus.go.
resolution: Added nostr.Keyer to DMBus, ControlRPCBus, dvm.Handler, NIP29/NIP28 channels; fixed all bunker-mode signing paths; all tests pass.

title: Add Keyer support to ControlRPCBus

resolution: Added nostr.Keyer to DMBus, ControlRPCBus, dvm.Handler, NIP29/NIP28 channels; fixed all bunker-mode signing paths; all tests pass.

resolution: Added nostr.Keyer to DMBus, ControlRPCBus, dvm.Handler, NIP29/NIP28 channels; fixed all bunker-mode signing paths; all tests pass.

title: Provider SDK migration & picoclaw feature adoption
description: Adopt official SDKs and proven patterns from picoclaw to replace hand-rolled HTTP code and improve error handling, model routing, and tool extensibility.
resolution: All 5 child tasks complete: (1) OpenAI SDK migration, (2) MCP client via go-sdk, (3) Features/RuleClassifier routing, (4) Comprehensive error classifier, (5) Copilot CLI SDK provider.

title: Migrate OpenAI provider to official openai-go SDK
description: Replace raw HTTP OpenAI code (OpenAIChatProviderChat, openAIRequest/Response types, SSE streaming parser) with the official openai/openai-go SDK. Same pattern as the anthropic-sdk-go migration: type-safe requests, automatic retries, native streaming, and the newer Responses API.
resolution: Migrated OpenAI provider to official openai-go SDK v3.29.0. Replaced raw HTTP with SDK client (option.WithBaseURL, option.WithAPIKey, option.WithHTTPClient). Rewrote chat_openai.go with typed SDK messages, Stream() with SDK streaming, removed ~300 lines of dead hand-rolled types from provider.go. All tests pass.

title: Add MCP client support via modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk
description: Integrate the official MCP Go SDK to connect to external MCP servers (stdio and HTTP transports). Register discovered MCP tools as agent tools. Support config-driven server definitions with env files, parallel startup, and graceful shutdown. Reference: picoclaw pkg/mcp/manager.go.
resolution: Added MCP client support via modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk v1.3.1. Created internal/mcp package with Manager (stdio+SSE/HTTP transport), config parsing from extra.mcp, tool adapter (MCPToolToToolDef) that maps MCP tools to swarmstr ToolFunc+ToolDefinition. Wired into main.go with deferred cleanup. 8 unit tests.

title: Adopt picoclaw's Features/RuleClassifier for model routing
description: Replace keyword-based scoreComplexity() with picoclaw's language-agnostic Features struct and RuleClassifier. Key improvements: CJK-aware token estimation, code block counting, recent tool call density from history, conversation depth scoring, attachment detection with hard-gate for multi-modal. This makes routing work correctly for all languages.
resolution: Replaced keyword-based scoreComplexity with picoclaw Features/RuleClassifier. Language-agnostic structural signals: CJK-aware token estimation, code block counting, tool call density, conversation depth, attachment detection. ModelRouter.SelectModel now accepts optional history. 14 tests.

title: Adopt picoclaw's comprehensive error classifier for fallback
description: Upgrade classifyError() in fallback.go with picoclaw's ~40-pattern error classifier from error_classifier.go. Adds: billing errors (402), image dimension/size errors, overloaded vs rate_limit distinction, transient status codes (500-529), detailed auth patterns, regex-based HTTP status extraction. Structured FailoverError with status code tracking.
resolution: Replaced basic 10-pattern error classifier with picoclaw/OpenClaw comprehensive ~40-pattern classifier. Added regex+substring patterns for rate limits, overloaded, billing, auth, format, and timeout. HTTP status code extraction (500/502/503/521-524/529 transient). Added billing reason with 10min cooldown. Context cancellation/deadline handling. 27 test cases.

title: Add GitHub Copilot SDK provider via copilot-sdk/go
description: Replace the current OpenAI-compat GitHub Copilot approach with the official github/copilot-sdk/go. Supports gRPC transport, proper session management, and native model selection. Reference: picoclaw pkg/providers/github_copilot_provider.go.
resolution: Added CopilotCLIChatProvider (chat_copilot.go) using github/copilot-sdk/go v0.1.32. Connects to local Copilot CLI server via JSON-RPC, lazy session init, auto-approve permissions. Registered as copilot-cli and copilot-cli/{model} in NewProviderForModel and BuildChatProviderForModel. COPILOT_CLI_URL env for external CLI server. 2 unit tests.
